description: A service believed to be highly available actually depends on a resource pinned to one availability zone, so a single zone outage takes it down.
body: Failure mode: compute is spread across AZs but a database, NAT gateway, or cache sits in one AZ, so when that zone fails the whole service fails despite the redundant front end. Detection signals: an architecture review finds a single-AZ resource in the critical path; a zone-failure game day causes a full outage; placement shows one AZ for a key dependency. Do not assume multi-AZ front end implies multi-AZ everywhere. Safer fallback: identify every resource in the request path, make stateful dependencies multi-AZ with tested failover, and rehearse a zone loss. Risk boundary: escalate an untested cross-AZ/region failover rather than claiming HA.
